front: Work: a cafe waiter whose movements are 'a little too precise, a little too rapid' illustrates this book's concept of bad faith (mauvaise foi)
Work: its section 'The Look' analyzes the objectifying phenomenon of being gazed at by others; it uses the example of seeing a mannequin
Work: a man walks into a cafe looking for his friend Pierre to illustrate the 'origin of negation' and nothingness
Concept: a late passage of Being and Nothingness calls this substance the 'revenge of the in-itself,' transcending all distinctions between psychic and physical
Work: Antoine Roquentin is overwhelmed by a chestnut tree and concludes he and his surroundings are 'superfluous' while trying to write a biography of the Marquis de Rollebon
Work: a pedophilic 'Self-Taught Man' named Ogier P reads library books in alphabetical order in the town of Bouville
Work: Garcin, Inez (a lesbian postal clerk), and Estelle are trapped in a room with Second Empire furnishings; ends with 'hell is other people'
Work: a woman stabs another with a paper-knife to show it has no effect; the room has a bronze ornament and a valet with no eyelids
Work: political prisoner Pablo Ibbieta gives what he thinks is false information about Ramon Gris hiding in a cemetery, but it turns out to be true
Work: Paul Hilbert, inspired by Herostratus's burning of the Temple of Artemis, plans to kill five people and commit suicide with his sixth bullet
Work: Eve refuses to send her insane husband Pierre to a clinic and vows to shoot him when he descends into total dementia
Philosopher: wrote the preface to Frantz Fanon's The Wretched of the Earth, stating 'the status of native is a nervous condition'
Philosopher: described the Negritude movement as 'anti-racist racism' in the essay 'Black Orpheus,' which opens Senghor's poetry anthology
Work: divides Jews into 'authentic' and 'inauthentic' forms; examines antisemitism through the lens of bad faith and denial of responsibility
Work: Hugo Barine takes the code-name Raskolnikov and murders the Illyrian socialist Hoederer for kissing his wife
Work: Senator Clarke convinces the sex worker Lizzie MacKay to falsely accuse a black man of assault; she receives a hundred-dollar bill instead of thanks
Work: a retelling of the Oresteia in which Orestes refuses to feel guilt for killing Clytemnestra; the ruler of Argos removes a boulder to let ghosts roam free
Philosopher: wrote The Family Idiot, a 2,800-page psychoanalytic study of Flaubert, and analyzed him via a 'regressive-progressive method' in Search for a Method
Philosopher: wrote Saint Genet (subtitled 'Actor and Martyr'), a study of Jean Genet deemed 'grotesquely verbose' by Susan Sontag
Philosopher: coined the term 'anti-novel' in his preface to Nathalie Sarraute's Portrait of a Man Unknown
Philosopher: Merleau-Ponty quit this thinker's journal Les Temps Modernes after the Korean War and criticized his 'Ultrabolshevism' in Adventures of the Dialectic
